note: The framing structure for the whole world beyond the Valley. GM-facing world-bible. The known world is the axis-space made land: twelve Reaches, each a region where one spectrum-axis runs strongest, arranged not as a flat map but as a coordinate-space you travel through. The Valley is ONE basin (a mechanical/spiritual + death/life + chaos/order crossing). Each Reach has a LOCUS (its heart, where the axis is purest and most dangerous), ENVIRONS (the lands around it, shading toward neighboring axes), a CIVILIZATION shaped by its axis, and a POWER-TRADITION rooted there. Skills come from the land: each Reach grows the crafts its axis makes possible. This is how the 12-axis skill breadth (SNG-016) resolves — not a taxonomy bolted on, but geography that already implies the skills. Travel between Reaches crosses MARCHES (contested blend-lands). Authored by Aevi (PO) as the SNG-016 direction proposal + world expansion.
principle: The world is a coordinate-space, and to travel it is to move through the axes. A character's cosmic address is literally a position among the Reaches. Propagation — the framework's motion-shape of being — is what walking the world IS. You do not leave the Valley to see more scenery; you leave it to move along an axis, and you become more of what that axis makes as you go. The far Reaches are dangerous the way the deep end of any axis is dangerous: pure death, pure order, pure abstraction each unmake a person who arrives unready.
the_valley_placement: The Valley of Echoes sits at a mild crossing near the world's center — mechanical/spiritual moderate, death/life moderate, chaos/order moderate. This is why it is habitable, why its crafts (resonance, radiance, greenlore, the Precursor deep) are what they are, and why it makes a good beginning: it is close to the origin of the coordinate-space, where the axes are gentle. Every direction out of the Valley leads toward a purer, harder Reach.

reaches.name: The Quickwood / The Palelands
reaches.civilization: The Rootkin at the living end — grove-cities that grow rather than build; the Ashwardens at the dying end, who tend endings as the Rootkin tend growth.
reaches.tradition: Vivimancy (the life-craft: healing, growth, quickening) and Palework (the death-craft: endings, decay-reading, the mercy of stopping). Thornwake Glade is a Valley-edge foothill of the living end.
reaches.crisis: The two poles are supposed to balance; something is pulling the whole Reach toward the Pale, and the Ashwardens are winning ground they don't want.
reaches.environs: Fungal deep-forests, bloom-fields that feed a continent, and the grey Hollow March where growth simply stops.

reaches.name: The Riven Marches / The Stillhold
reaches.civilization: The Marchers — a warrior culture at the violent end whose whole society is honed conflict; the Stillhold, a walled peace so absolute it has become its own kind of prison.
reaches.tradition: The Edge (violence-craft: the martial disciplines, the arts of force) and the Stillcraft (peace-craft: de-escalation, binding, the making of true truces). The Valley's Mediator's Tongue is a folk-shadow of Stillcraft.
reaches.crisis: The Redline is bleeding outward; the Stillhold's peace is revealed to rest on something terrible, and holding it may be worse than the war it prevents.
reaches.environs: Battle-plains, fortress-marches, and the unnerving quiet of a land where no violence has occurred in living memory and no one remembers why.

reaches.name: The Somatic Reaches / The Cogitarium
reaches.civilization: The Somatics — cultures of the perfected body, movement as language; the Cogitants, who have nearly left their bodies behind for pure thought and pay for it.
reaches.tradition: Soma (body-craft: the physical masteries, breath, endurance, the body as instrument) and Noesis (mind-craft: the mental disciplines, memory-palaces, thought as force). Currently the Valley has almost none of either — this Reach is where they live.
reaches.crisis: The Cogitarium is losing its bodies to its own discipline; the Flesh-Temples reject thought so hard they are forgetting how to warn each other. Each Reach is dying of too much of itself.
reaches.environs: Training-cities, the floating thought-halls of the Cogitarium, and the border where embodied and disembodied cultures fail to understand one another.

reaches.name: The Unspooling / The Lattice-Cities
reaches.civilization: The Churnfolk, who live in constant productive disorder; the Lattice-Cities, perfectly ordered arcologies where nothing is unplanned and nothing is free.
reaches.tradition: Wildcraft (chaos-craft: improvisation, luck-riding, the arts of the unplanned) and Latticework (order-craft: structure, law, prediction, the binding of possibility). The Valley's tinkering and its factions are the gentle middle of this axis.
reaches.crisis: The Grand Lattice is expanding its perfect order outward and calling it peace; the Churn is the last place it can't predict, and the Churn knows it's next.
reaches.environs: Ever-shifting chaos-territories that are never mapped the same twice, and the crystalline order-cities where the axis's cold perfection shows what the Precursor sealed-thing might become.

reaches.name: The Umbral Depths / The Radiant Wastes
reaches.civilization: The Umbrals, who see by other means in the great dark; the Blazeborn, who live in a light so total it has burned their land to glass. Radiant Plateau is a Valley-edge foothill of the light end.
reaches.tradition: Umbracraft (dark-craft: the arts of the hidden, the unseen, sight-without-light) and Radiance (light-craft: revelation, the beam, the burning truth). Prism Sight is folk-Radiance.
reaches.crisis: The Blaze is expanding and sterilizing; the Underlight harbors what the light would destroy, including things that should perhaps be destroyed. Neither pole is innocent.
reaches.environs: Sunless underworlds lit by living glow, and glass deserts where the light has won and nothing else survives.

reaches.name: The Gearlands / The Numinous Reach
reaches.civilization: The Enginewrights, whose entire world is mechanism; the Numinous, who have dissolved most of their material life into pure spirit-practice. Harmonic Heights is a Valley-edge foothill leaning mechanical.
reaches.tradition: Enginecraft (mechanical-craft: machines, systems, the Precursor arts at their most technical) and Numenwork (spiritual-craft: the practices of pure meaning, the mystic disciplines the Valley's framework keeps converging on). The Precursor deep belongs to this axis most of all.
reaches.crisis: The Great Engine is waking old Precursor works the Numen say must stay asleep; this is the Reach where What Sleeps Under has its true home, and the Valley's water crisis is a tremor from here.
reaches.environs: Machine-continents that run without people, and the thinning Numinous lands where the material world becomes negotiable.

remaining_reaches_note: Six Reaches sketched above (the axes most in need of skill-homes + the ones the Valley already touches). The other six — emotional/logical, falsehood/truth, demonic/angelic, concrete/abstract, space/time, destruction/creation — are named-but-unbuilt frontier, to author as the world opens. Each will follow the same shape: two poles, two civilizations, two crafts, a crisis, and environs. Deliberately left as horizon so the world always has undiscovered country.
marches: Between any two Reaches lies a March — a blend-land where both axes are moderate and the two civilizations meet, trade, and grate. Marches are where most cross-Reach play happens; they are habitable the way the Valley is habitable, because no single axis runs pure there. The Valley itself is effectively a triple-March near the world's origin.
how_skills_resolve: SNG-016 answer: abilities are not assigned to axes abstractly — they are the crafts of the Reaches. A player gains access to a Reach's tradition by traveling there, training under its masters, or carrying its influence. The gap-axes (body/mind, violence/peace, death/life as full traditions) fill because their Reaches now exist as places you can go. The Valley's four systems are the gentle folk-crafts of the central Marches; the deep traditions live at the poles, purer and more perilous. This keeps the Valley as a complete starting experience while making the wider world a real, walkable expansion — and it makes leveling geographic: you grow by moving through the coordinate-space, which is the framework's propagation made literal.
